extraction_warnings: CRITICAL: Q10 (multi-select: what can a DT do?) not extracted as multi-select. WS11_L10_1 through L10_8 appear to be Likert attitude ratings (1-5 scale), NOT Q10 cognitive items. These are different question types on the same worksheet.
CRITICAL: Q11 (algorithm step ordering: 1-4 sequence) not captured at all.
CRITICAL: Q12 (mark all that apply: which of these is AI?) not captured at all.
B9 contains a DT definition — this IS the expected content for Q9 (define karar agaci in your own words). B9 is correctly extracted.
L11 and L12 items are Likert scales and demographic fields respectively — correctly captured.

items.WS11_B1: Karar ağacı kavramını daha iyi anladım. Özellikle eşik değerinin nasıl seçildiğini öğrendim.
items.WS11_B2: WS5 en çok zaman aldı ama en çok şeyi de orada öğrendim.
items.WS11_B3: Duyarlılık ve MCR formüllerini başta karıştırdım, sonunda netleşti.
items.WS11_B4: Ağaç çizmek düşündüğümden zordu; dalları doğru yönde etiketlemek dikkat istedi.
items.WS11_B5: Gerçek veriyle çalışmak soyut kavramları somutlaştırdı.
items.WS11_B6: Daha fazla örnek veri seti üzerinde pratik yapmak isterdim.
items.WS11_B7: Bu etkinliği başka derslerde de görmek isterdim.
items.WS11_B9: Karar ağacı, verideki örüntüleri öğrenerek yeni örnekleri sınıflandıran, dal ve yaprak düğümlerden oluşan bir makine öğrenmesi modelidir.
items.WS11_Q10_multiselect: (not_captured — needs re-extraction as checkbox selection, not Likert)
items.WS11_Q12_ai_identification: (not_captured — multi-select AI identification task not extracted)

item_schema.WS11_B1_B7: Open-ended reflections: what I learned, which WS was hardest, what I struggled with, what helped, what I would change
item_schema.WS11_B8a: Apply student's own rule: Çikolata (sugar=15g) → classification using student's WS6 tree (şeker > 10 → Tavsiye edilmez)
item_schema.WS11_B8b: Write the rule used for B8a in if-then form
item_schema.WS11_B9: Q9: Define 'karar ağacı' in your own words
item_schema.WS11_Q10_multiselect: Q10: Check all statements that a DT CAN do. Correct selections: {1,2,4,5,8}. Wrong: {3,6,7}. Not captured.
item_schema.WS11_Q11_ordering: Q11: Order the 4 algorithm steps correctly. Expected order: 1=feature selection, 2=sort data, 3=find threshold, 4=classify. Not captured.
item_schema.WS11_Q12_ai_identification: Q12: Which of these is AI? Correct: {1,5} (learning systems). Not captured.
item_schema.WS11_L10_1_to_L10_8: Likert scale (1-5): attitude toward activity items 1-8
item_schema.WS11_L11_1_to_L11_3: Likert scale (1-5): course integration attitude items 1-3

raw_ocr.WS11_B1: Karar ağacı kavramını daha iyi anladım. Özellikle eşik değerinin nasıl seçildiğini öğrendim.
raw_ocr.WS11_B2: WS5 en çok zaman aldı ama en çok şeyi de orada öğrendim.
raw_ocr.WS11_B3: Duyarlılık ve MCR formüllerini başta karıştırdım, sonunda netleşti.
raw_ocr.WS11_B4: Ağaç çizmek düşündüğümden zordu; dalları doğru yönde etiketlemek dikkat istedi.
raw_ocr.WS11_B5: Gerçek veriyle çalışmak soyut kavramları somutlaştırdı.
raw_ocr.WS11_B6: Daha fazla örnek veri seti üzerinde pratik yapmak isterdim.
raw_ocr.WS11_B7: Bu etkinliği başka derslerde de görmek isterdim.
raw_ocr.WS11_B9: Karar ağacı, verideki örüntüleri öğrenerek yeni örnekleri sınıflandıran, dal ve yaprak düğümlerden oluşan bir makine öğrenmesi modelidir.
raw_ocr.ws_snapshot: Sample_Student tüm bölümleri eksiksiz doldurmuş. Likert yanıtları genel olarak yüksek (4-5 arası); etkinliğe olumlu yaklaşmış. B9'daki karar ağacı tanımı kavramsal olarak doğru ve öz. B8b'deki kural ifadesi doğru.

student_profile_from_demographics.note: Pre-service primary mathematics teacher, year 3, no programming background. Important context for interpreting AI-CFT level: Acquire is the realistic target for this profile.

warnings: Q10, Q11, Q12 cognitive items not captured — these are the most direct measures of conceptual understanding in WS11.
L10 Likert items are attitude measures, not cognitive performance — do not use for AI-CFT level.

student_snapshot.llm_observations: High engagement visible in reflections (B1-B7). Student spontaneously identifies WS5 as most informative, confirms sensitivity/MCR confusion resolved. B8a/b correctly applies own tree rule to new example. B9 DT definition is strong. Likert scores consistently 4-5.

WS11_B8a.rationale: Student correctly applies own tree rule to Çikolata. Çikolata has sugar=15g > 10g (student's threshold). Rule: şeker > 10 → Tavsiye edilmez. Student answered 'Tavsiye edilmez'. Correct.

WS11_B8b.rationale: If-then rule correctly formulated: 'Eğer şeker > 10 ise → Tavsiye edilmez.' Form is correct (if-then), condition is correct (şeker > 10), outcome is correct (Tavsiye edilmez). Consistent with WS6 tree right branch and WS7 B3.

WS11_B9.rationale: DT definition contains both required elements: (1) 'verideki örüntüleri öğrenerek' — learning from data, (2) 'yeni örnekleri sınıflandıran' — classifies new examples, (3) 'dal ve yaprak düğümlerden oluşan' — tree structure, (4) 'makine öğrenmesi modelidir' — places within ML context. This is a complete and correct definition.
WS11_B9.evidence_quote: Karar ağacı, verideki örüntüleri öğrenerek yeni örnekleri sınıflandıran, dal ve yaprak düğümlerden oluşan bir makine öğrenmesi modelidir.

WS11_Q10_multiselect.rationale: Q10 multi-select not extracted. Answer key: correct selections are {1,2,4,5,8} (things DT can do). Wrong: {3,6,7}. Cannot score without extraction.

WS11_Q11_ordering.rationale: Q11 step ordering not extracted. Expected correct order: 1=feature selection, 2=sort data, 3=find threshold, 4=classify new example. Cannot score.

WS11_Q12_ai_identification.rationale: Q12 multi-select not extracted. Expected correct selections: {1,5} (systems that learn). Cannot score.

WS11_reflections_B1_B7.rationale: Reflections not scored for correctness — they are self-report data. Qualitative observations: (1) Student identifies WS5 as high-effort and high-learning, which aligns with our assessment that WS5 is the strongest Deepen activity. (2) Student acknowledges prior confusion with sensitivity/MCR formulas — consistent with WS1_B6 partial credit. (3) Student identifies 'tree branch labeling' as difficult — consistent with WS6 B12 blank. These reflections corroborate the scoring pattern.
WS11_reflections_B1_B7.evidence_quote: WS5 en çok zaman aldı ama en çok şeyi de orada öğrendim. / Duyarlılık ve MCR formüllerini başta karıştırdım, sonunda netleşti.
WS11_reflections_B1_B7.flag: qualitative — not counted in credit totals

WS11_Likert_L10_L11.rationale: Likert attitude items (1-5 scale) are research instrument data, not cognitive performance items. Mean L10 = 4.25, mean L11 = 4.33. Indicates positive attitude toward the activity. Not used for AI-CFT level.

summary.scoring_note: B8a, B8b, B9 all full credit — strong application and definition. Q10/Q11/Q12 not captured — these are the most direct cognitive assessment items on WS11 and must be re-extracted. Reflections qualitatively corroborate scoring pattern from other worksheets.

Acquire_confirmed: WS11_B8a: Correctly applies own tree rule to new unseen example (Çikolata) — LO3.1.1 (applying classification rule to new data)
WS11_B8b: Correctly formulates if-then rule — LO3.1.1
WS11_B9: Complete and correct DT definition including data-learning aspect and tree structure — LO3.1.2
Acquire_missing: Q10 (DT capabilities), Q11 (algorithm steps), Q12 (AI identification) — not captured, cannot confirm or deny

indicators.LO3.1.5: approaching — Q12 would confirm but not extracted

evidence.student_profile_note: Pre-service primary math teacher, no programming background, year 3. Acquire as final level is appropriate and meaningful for this profile. The reflections show metacognitive awareness of own learning gaps (formula confusion) and strengths (WS5 as peak learning moment).

gate_4_aicft.ws_contribution_to_portfolio: WS11 B8a/b confirm transfer: student applies own tree rule to a genuinely new example (Çikolata was not in the WS6 dataset). B9 shows DT understanding is retained as genuine definition, not just rote formula. Q10/Q11/Q12 gaps mean the full cognitive profile is incomplete — re-extraction needed.
